tasks.title: Project Setup Phase
subtasks: Create a new Python file named factorial.py
Set up the basic script structure including imports and main function
Review requirements and assumptions to ensure alignment
tasks.test_procedure: Verify that the file is created, can be executed in a command-line environment, and runs without errors initially.

tasks.title: Implementation Phase
subtasks: Write code to prompt the user for input as per REQ-001
Implement the recursive factorial function as per REQ-002
Add error handling for negative numbers as per REQ-003
Add error handling for non-integer inputs as per REQ-004
tasks.test_procedure: Manually test the function with valid inputs (e.g., 5) to ensure factorial is calculated correctly, and with invalid inputs (e.g., -1 or 'abc') to check for appropriate error messages without crashing.

tasks.title: Output Formatting Phase
subtasks: Format the output to display results clearly as per REQ-005, e.g., 'The factorial of 5 is 120'
Ensure error messages are user-friendly, e.g., 'Error: Input must be a non-negative integer'
tasks.test_procedure: Run the script with various inputs and verify that the output is readable and meets the success criteria for clarity.

tasks.title: Testing and Validation Phase
subtasks: Write unit tests for the factorial function and error handling
Perform manual testing for edge cases like 0, 1, and large numbers within recursion limits
Validate against success criteria: correct computation, error handling, and clear output
tasks.test_procedure: Execute tests in a command-line environment and confirm that all requirements are met without issues.

tasks.title: Finalization and Deployment Phase
subtasks: Review and refactor code for cleanliness
Ensure the script runs in a standard command-line environment as per assumptions
Document the code with comments for maintainability
tasks.test_procedure: Run the full script multiple times to ensure integration works, and package if needed for deployment.
